pairs.question: Which club won the Premier League title in the 1994–95 season?
pairs.answer: Blackburn Rovers F.C. won the Premier League title in the 1994–95 season, securing their first English league title in 81 years.

pairs.question: How many points did Blackburn Rovers finish ahead of Manchester United in the 1994–95 Premier League?
pairs.answer: Blackburn Rovers finished just one point ahead of Manchester United in the 1994–95 Premier League standings.

pairs.question: What significant event occurred on the final day of the 1994–95 Premier League season involving Blackburn Rovers?
pairs.answer: Blackburn Rovers suffered a 2–1 defeat at Liverpool's Anfield, briefly raising concerns about their title hopes before Manchester United's draw at West Ham secured the title for Rovers.

pairs.question: Who was the manager of Blackburn Rovers during their 1994–95 Premier League title-winning season?
pairs.answer: Kenny Dalglish managed Blackburn Rovers during their 1994–95 Premier League title-winning season and subsequently won the Premier League Manager of the Year award.

pairs.question: List the key achievements of Alan Shearer during the 1994–95 season.
pairs.answer: Alan Shearer scored 34 league goals (contributing to Blackburn's total of 80 goals), won the Golden Boot, and was named the PFA Players' Player of the Year for his performances in the 1994–95 season.

pairs.question: How long did Jack Walker take to transform Blackburn Rovers from Second Division strugglers to Premier League champions?
pairs.answer: Jack Walker achieved this transformation within five years of acquiring the club, elevating them from strugglers in the old Second Division to champions of England.

pairs.question: Which players from Blackburn Rovers were named in the PFA Team of the Year for the 1994–95 season?
pairs.answer: The Blackburn Rovers players named in the PFA Team of the Year for the 1994–95 season were Tim Flowers, Graeme Le Saux, Colin Hendry, Tim Sherwood, Chris Sutton, and Alan Shearer.
